cases.title: Hann v. Pee Dee Presbytery
topics: complainant not subject to presbytery jurisdiction
cases.synopsis: The SJC dismissed Mrs. Hann's complaint against Pee Dee Presbytery because she lacked standing, not being subject to the jurisdiction of the Presbytery. The ruling noted that Presbytery's ongoing investigation was not stayed by this ruling.

cases.title: Reynolds v. Pee Dee Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC dismissed Mrs. Reynolds's complaint against Pee Dee Presbytery as untimely, finding it was filed more than sixty days after the Session action in question. The ruling expressly reserved questions about whether she might bring charges as an injured party under BCO 31-5.

cases.title: Biese et al. v. Tennessee Valley Presbytery

parties.complainant_or_appellant: TE Ryan Biese, RE Nathan Bowers, RE Wil Davis (et al.)

cases.synopsis: Tennessee Valley Presbytery approved without exception Session minutes planning a youth group Super Bowl Party on Sunday; the SJC denied the complaint because the record was insufficient to find Presbytery erred in its constitutional interpretation. A dissent by RE Eggert argued the complaint was judicially out of order because the SJC lacks constitutional authority to review a presbytery's session-records review decisions under BCO 40-2 and 40-3.

cases.title: Baird et al. v. Grace Presbytery

parties.complainant_or_appellant: TE Knox Baird et al. (Session members of First Presbyterian Church, Hattiesburg, MS)

cases.synopsis: The SJC sustained multiple complaints brought by elders of First Presbyterian Church Hattiesburg against Grace Presbytery's Commission and Committee, finding: the Blue Hen complaint was moot and never properly filed; the Commission improperly directed the Clerk to produce personal communications beyond official records; Presbytery improperly directed the Session to inform the congregation; and the Commission's stay of pending trials was unconstitutional because BCO 40-5 requires citation of the lower court before any stay can be imposed. Issue 5 (appointment of Committee for BCO 40-5 Reports) was denied.
